techniques.name: Active Scanning
techniques.description: The adversary probes victim infrastructure via network traffic, e.g. scanning IP blocks or ports, to gather information for targeting.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: The adversary exploits a weakness in an Internet-facing application (web app, database, service) to gain initial access.

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: The adversary sends phishing messages (malicious attachments or links) to gain access to victim systems.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: The adversary obtains and abuses credentials of existing accounts for access, persistence or privilege escalation.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: The adversary abuses command and script interpreters (PowerShell, cmd, bash, Python) to execute commands or payloads.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: The adversary abuses task scheduling functionality (scheduled tasks, cron) to execute or persist malicious code.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
techniques.description: The adversary configures system settings (registry run keys, startup folder) to execute a program automatically at boot or logon.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: The adversary manipulates accounts (adding privileges, credentials or groups) to maintain or elevate access.

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: The adversary injects code into live processes to evade defenses or elevate privileges.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: The adversary disables or tampers with security tools (antivirus, logging, firewall) to avoid detection.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: The adversary attempts to gain access by systematically guessing credentials (brute force, password spraying, credential stuffing).

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping
techniques.description: The adversary dumps credential material from the operating system (LSASS memory, SAM, NTDS) to obtain account logins.

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.description: The adversary enumerates services running on remote hosts to identify targets for lateral movement or exploitation.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: The adversary uses valid accounts over remote services (RDP, SMB, SSH, WinRM) to move laterally between systems.

techniques.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
techniques.description: The adversary transfers tools or files between systems inside a compromised environment to support lateral movement.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: The adversary communicates with command-and-control servers over application layer protocols (HTTP(S), DNS, mail) to blend with normal traffic.

techniques.name: Dynamic Resolution
techniques.description: The adversary dynamically establishes command-and-control infrastructure, e.g. via domain generation algorithms or fast flux DNS.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: The adversary transfers tools or files from an external system into the compromised environment.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
techniques.description: The adversary exfiltrates stolen data over the existing command-and-control channel.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: The adversary exfiltrates data to an external web service (cloud storage, code repositories) rather than the primary C2 channel.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: The adversary encrypts data on target systems to interrupt availability, typically demanding ransom for decryption.
